name: Kotmis Satsivi
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Walnuts, shelled and pulverized into a paste
2 tablespoons Red wine vinegar or cider vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Dry the chicken inside and out.
2. Melt the butter and oil together, then brush the chicken with this mixture until completely coated.
3. Preheat the oven to 475 degrees F (245 degrees C).
4. Place the chicken on its side on a rack in a shallow baking pan. Roast for 10 minutes, then turn the chicken onto its other side and brush again with the butter-oil mixture. Continue roasting for another 10 minutes.
5. Turn the bird onto its back and reduce the oven temperature to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Baste the chicken with the butter-oil mixture, then roast for another 40 minutes. Baste at least once during this time period.
6. While the chicken is roasting, prepare the walnut sauce:
a. Melt the butter in a large, heavy skillet over high heat.
b. Add the onions and garlic, lower the heat to medium, and cook while stirring for 4 minutes.
c. Stir in the flour and mix into a paste.
d. Stir in the chicken stock, bring to a boil over high heat, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens.
e. Stir in the vinegar, cloves, salt, pepper, bay leaf, saffron, parsley, and walnut paste. Lower the heat to low and simmer for 6 minutes.
7. Quarter the chicken and pour the sauce over the quarters.
8. Serve immediately.
ORIGIN: Chef Marina Chekov, Hotel Otrar, Almaty, Kazakhstan
